meaning: n.中庸,知能学识平凡的人adj.极普通的
examples: This programme disproves the theory that TV, a middlebrow medium, is neither as smart as the smartest films nor as dumb as the dumbest.
In the middle of all this, the middlebrow general-interest magazines have tried to adapt.
Like Norah Jones8, Austen seems to offer middlebrow entertainment with an up-market sheen9.
With markups of just over 10%--most middlebrow retailers are closer to 30%--Costco is able to introduce its aspiring multitudes to increasingly upscale tastes.
She should ask him point blank if and why he thinks her views are middlebrow, and what exactly that is supposed to mean.
